name: Optical stack
nameVi: Quang học (giác mạc → thủy tinh thể)
oneLineJob: Get light to the back of the eye, in focus.

cascadesTo.mechanism: cataract or vitreous haze can mask retinal pathology

name: Sensor (retina)
oneLineJob: Convert focused light into a neural signal.

cascadesTo.mechanism: ganglion axons exit via the optic disc; severe sensor loss reduces what transport carries

name: Transport (optic nerve & pathway)
oneLineJob: Carry the signal from eye to visual cortex intact.

name: Coordination (binocular)
oneLineJob: Aim both eyes at the same target and fuse the two images.

cascadesTo.mechanism: CN III carries both 4 EOMs and pupil constrictor; lesions affect both
cascadesTo.exampleDisease: CN III palsy with blown pupil

name: Surface (tear film, lids, conjunctiva)
oneLineJob: Keep the front of the eye wet, smooth, and defended.

cascadesTo.mechanism: broken tear film blurs vision and corrupts topography / biometry

name: Pressure (aqueous dynamics)
oneLineJob: Keep the globe inflated at the right pressure (IOP 10–21 mmHg).

cascadesTo.mechanism: lamina cribrosa deforms under high pressure, pinching axons

name: Vascular supply
oneLineJob: Deliver oxygen and nutrients to retina and optic nerve; clear waste.

cascadesTo.mechanism: barrier breakdown causes edema; ischemia causes infarct
posterior ciliary supply to disc fails
VEGF-driven new vessels grow in the angle

name: Immune (defense and inflammation)
oneLineJob: Defend the eye against infection and debris without damaging clear tissue.

cascadesTo.mechanism: cells and flare in AC; inflammatory cataract
meshwork inflammation or steroid-induced IOP rise

name: Autonomic (pupil, accommodation, lid tone)
oneLineJob: Auto-tune pupil size, focusing, and lid tone involuntarily.

cascadesTo.mechanism: mydriatic drops in a narrow angle precipitate acute closure
